title: Declare AI capability and boundaries before reliance
summary: Every AI surface states the supported task, important limits, data/tool boundary, freshness and expected latency before users depend on it.
guidance: Describe observable capability in user language. Disclose provider/model/version only where it changes assessment or accountability. Re-evaluate the contract when tools, data, model or autonomy change.
do: Show unsupported scope before or at the decision point
dont: Promise general intelligence from a narrow workflow
Use an AI badge as capability disclosure
Hide material data or latency constraints
verification: Contract review plus unsupported-task, missing-tool, stale-data and changed-model test cases
